title: Zakon o poslovnoj tajni
description: Zakon o poslovnoj tajni (ZPT) uspostavlja sveobuhvatan pravni okvir za zaštitu poslovnih tajni u Republici Srbiji, usklađen sa Direktivom (EU) 2016/943 o zaštiti poverljivog znanja i poverljivih poslovnih informacija (poslovnih tajni) od njihovog nezakonitog pribavljanja, korišćenja i otkrivanja. Zakon definiše poslovne tajne, zakonite i nezakonite postupke u pogledu poslovnih tajni i propisuje građanskopravne pravne lekove za nosioce prava. Takođe štiti zviždače i slobodu izražavanja.

provisions.title: Član 1. Predmet zakona
provisions.content: This Law establishes the legal framework for the protection of trade secrets against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure in the Republic of Serbia, modelled on Directive (EU) 2016/943 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure. The Law defines trade secrets, establishes the distinction between lawful and unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure of trade secrets, provides civil remedies for holders of trade secrets whose rights have been infringed, and protects freedom of expression, public interest disclosures, and employee mobility. The Law is part of Serbia's alignment with the EU acquis communautaire.

provisions.title: Član 2. Pojmovi (Definitions)
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 'trade secret' means information which meets all of the following requirements: it is secret in the sense that it is not, as a body or in the precise configuration and assembly of its components, generally known among or readily accessible to persons within the circles that normally deal with the kind of information in question; it has commercial value because it is secret; and it has been subject to reasonable steps under the circumstances, by the person lawfully in control of the information, to keep it secret. 'Trade secret holder' means any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret. 'Infringer' means any person who has unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed a trade secret.

provisions.title: Član 3. Zakonito pribavljanje (Lawful Acquisition)
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret shall be considered lawful when carried out by means of independent discovery or creation, observation, study, disassembly, or testing of a product or object that has been made available to the public or is lawfully in the possession of the acquirer who is free from any legally valid duty to limit the acquisition of the trade secret. Acquisition is also lawful when carried out through exercise of the right of workers or workers' representatives to information and consultation in accordance with applicable labour law, or through any other practice which, under the circumstances, is in conformity with honest commercial practices. Disclosure of a trade secret for the purpose of revealing a violation of law, including misconduct, fraud, or illegal activity, shall be lawful where carried out in the general public interest.

provisions.title: Član 4. Nezakonito pribavljanje (Unlawful Acquisition)
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret without the consent of the trade secret holder shall be considered unlawful when it results from: unauthorised access to, or copying of, documents, objects, materials, substances, or electronic files that are lawfully under the control of the trade secret holder and contain the trade secret or from which the trade secret can be deduced; theft, bribery, deception, breach of confidentiality or inducement to breach, or any other conduct which, under the circumstances, is contrary to honest commercial practices. The use or disclosure of a trade secret is unlawful where carried out without consent by a person who acquired the trade secret unlawfully, breached a confidentiality agreement, or is in breach of a contractual or other duty to limit its use.

provisions.title: Član 5. Izuzeci (Exceptions)
provisions.content: Measures, procedures, and remedies provided for in this Law shall not be applied where the alleged acquisition, use, or disclosure was carried out for the purpose of exercising the right to freedom of expression and information as guaranteed by the Constitution of the Republic of Serbia and applicable law; for the purpose of revealing a violation of law or other misconduct, wrongdoing, or illegal activity, provided the respondent acted for the purpose of protecting the general public interest; for disclosure by workers to their representatives as part of the legitimate exercise of their representative functions; or for protecting a legitimate interest recognised by applicable law. These exceptions shall be assessed in the light of the specific circumstances of each case.

provisions.title: Član 6. Građanskopravni lekovi (Civil Remedies)
provisions.content: Trade secret holders whose rights have been infringed may apply to competent courts for civil remedies including: the cessation of or prohibition on the use or disclosure of the trade secret on a provisional or permanent basis; the prohibition on the production, offering, placing on the market or import and export of infringing goods; the adoption of appropriate corrective measures with regard to infringing goods including their destruction or delivery up; the destruction or delivery up of documents, objects, materials, substances, or electronic files containing or embodying the trade secret; or any other appropriate measure the court considers necessary. Courts shall assess the proportionality of remedies having regard to the specific circumstances of each case.

provisions.title: Član 7. Privremene mere (Interim Measures)
provisions.content: On application by the trade secret holder, competent courts may order interim measures against an alleged infringer, including the immediate cessation of use or disclosure of the trade secret on a provisional basis, the prohibition of production or marketing of goods allegedly incorporating or derived from unlawfully acquired trade secrets, or the seizure of such goods. Courts may require the applicant to provide security or an equivalent assurance sufficient to prevent abuse and to ensure the alleged infringer is compensated for any prejudice suffered if the application is ultimately dismissed. Courts shall balance the interests of both parties and the public interest in determining interim measures.

provisions.title: Član 8. Naknada štete (Damages)
provisions.content: Infringers who knew or ought to have known that they were engaging in unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a trade secret shall be liable to pay damages appropriate to the actual prejudice suffered by the trade secret holder. In setting damages, courts shall take into account all appropriate factors, including the negative economic consequences, including lost profits, suffered by the trade secret holder, any unfair profits made by the infringer, and, in appropriate cases, elements other than economic factors such as moral prejudice to the trade secret holder. Courts may set damages as a lump sum based on elements such as the royalties that would have been due if authorisation had been sought.

provisions.title: Član 9. Poverljivost u sudskim postupcima
provisions.content: Competent courts may take appropriate measures to preserve the confidentiality of any trade secret or alleged trade secret used or referred to in the course of legal proceedings relating to the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of a trade secret. Measures may include restricting access to documents or hearings to a limited number of persons and imposing obligations of confidentiality on persons permitted access. Restrictions on access to hearings or court documents shall be balanced against the principle of open justice and shall be limited to what is strictly necessary to protect the confidentiality of the trade secret. Court decisions relating to trade secrets shall be published with appropriate redactions to protect confidential information.

provisions.title: Član 10. Zaštita zviždača (Whistleblower Protection)
provisions.content: This Law shall be applied in a manner that does not restrict the protection afforded to whistleblowers under applicable Serbian law, including the Law on Whistleblower Protection. Measures, procedures, and remedies shall not be applied against persons who disclosed a trade secret for the purpose of protecting the general public interest, where the disclosure revealed misconduct, fraud, or illegal activity, the respondent acted without intent of obtaining economic advantage, and the disclosure did not go beyond what was strictly necessary. Courts shall give particular weight to the public interest in transparency and accountability when assessing applications for remedies in cases involving alleged public interest disclosures.

provisions.title: Član 11. Mobilnost zaposlenih (Employee Mobility)
provisions.content: This Law shall not restrict the mobility of employees and shall not be applied so as to prevent employees from using the knowledge, skills, and experience honestly acquired in the normal course of their employment. Post-employment restrictions on the use of information by former employees shall be governed by applicable labour law and shall not be extended beyond what is proportionate and necessary to protect the legitimate business interests of the employer. Courts shall take account of employees' right to use their general knowledge and professional skills and the public interest in the free mobility of workers when considering applications for remedies against former employees relating to the use of information.

provisions.title: Član 12. Zastarelost (Limitation Period)
provisions.content: Claims for civil remedies under this Law shall be subject to a limitation period of three years from the date on which the trade secret holder became aware, or had reason to become aware, of the last act of unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of the trade secret giving rise to the claim. The limitation period shall be suspended or interrupted in accordance with the general rules of Serbian civil procedure law applicable to civil claims. For continuing or repeated acts of unlawful use or disclosure, each act shall give rise to a separate cause of action, and the limitation period shall run separately in respect of each such act.

provisions.title: Član 13. Sudska nadležnost (Jurisdiction)
provisions.content: The commercial courts of the Republic of Serbia shall have jurisdiction over claims for civil remedies under this Law where the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of the trade secret occurred within Serbian territory, where the defendant is domiciled or established in Serbia, or where Serbian law governs the applicable non-contractual obligation under applicable private international law rules. In cross-border cases involving defendants established in other jurisdictions, Serbian courts shall apply applicable international private law rules to determine jurisdiction and the applicable law. The recognition and enforcement of foreign judgments in trade secrets cases shall be subject to applicable bilateral or multilateral arrangements.

provisions.title: Član 14. Odnos prema pravima intelektualne svojine
provisions.content: The protection of trade secrets under this Law applies without prejudice to the protection of intellectual property rights, including patents, plant variety rights, designs, trademarks, and copyright, which are governed by applicable intellectual property legislation. Where information constituting a trade secret is also protected by intellectual property rights, the holder may rely on either or both forms of protection. The trade secrets framework applies independently of whether the information could in principle be protected by intellectual property rights, as long as the criteria for trade secret protection established in this Law are met.

provisions.title: Član 15. Usklađenost sa EU (EU Alignment)
provisions.content: This Law is modelled on Directive (EU) 2016/943 on the protection of trade secrets and is intended to align Serbian law with EU standards as part of the EU accession process. The Law shall be interpreted in a manner consistent with the Directive and with applicable case law of the Court of Justice of the EU, to the extent relevant to Serbian proceedings. The competent ministry shall monitor EU developments relating to trade secrets protection and shall propose updates to this Law where necessary to maintain alignment. Serbia aims to achieve full alignment with EU trade secrets law upon accession.

provisions.title: Član 16. Savesnost i zaštita trećih lica
provisions.content: Where a person acquires goods that they know or ought to know were produced using unlawfully acquired trade secrets, that person shall also be subject to the remedies available under this Law in respect of their use of those goods. A person who acquires a trade secret in good faith shall not be liable under this Law unless and until they become aware of the unlawful origin of the information. Courts shall take account of the good faith of the defendant and of any actions taken following discovery of the unlawful origin of the trade secret in determining the appropriate remedy. The burden of demonstrating good faith shall rest on the person claiming it.

definitions.definition: Information that is secret (not generally known or readily accessible), has commercial value because it is secret, and has been subject to reasonable steps to keep it secret by the person lawfully controlling it.
Any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret.
Any person who has unlawfully acquired, used, or disclosed a trade secret.

Standards of conduct consistent with fair and legitimate business practice in the relevant commercial context, assessed objectively in the light of the circumstances of the acquisition or use of the information.
